title: On the Danger of Certainty
author: From: The Book of Five Rings by Miyamoto Musashi
content: Do not have any favorite weapon. To become over-reliant on one tool or one method is to become predictable, and thus vulnerable.
action: Action: Temporarily abandon your favorite productivity tool or method for 24 hours to test your adaptability.

title: On the Architecture of Silence
author: From: The Tao Te Ching
content: He who knows does not speak. He who speaks does not know. True leadership is found in observation, not in the noise of command.
action: Action: In your next meeting, try to speak 50% less than usual and focus entirely on observing team dynamics.

title: The Availability Heuristic
author: From: Thinking, Fast and Slow by Daniel Kahneman
content: We judge the frequency or probability of an event by how easily examples come to mind. Don't let your 'recent' memories dictate your 'long-term' strategy.
action: Action: List three facts about your project that contradict your current 'feeling' about it.

title: The 5 Whys
author: From: The Toyota Way
content: Surface-level problems are just symptoms. Ask 'why' five times to reach the root cause.
action: Action: Take one recurring problem you face and run the '5 Whys' on it.

title: The Gambler’s Fallacy
author: From: Probability Theory
content: The past does not influence the probability of a future independent event. Each decision is a clean slate.
action: Action: Don't let a 'streak' of success or failure influence your next tactical decision.
